name: Papeete
summary: Papeete is the busy capital of French Polynesia, sitting on Tahiti, the biggest island in this group of South Pacific islands. Green volcanic mountains rise behind the town and bright blue lagoons sparkle in front.
funFacts: Tahiti was made by underwater volcanoes, and Mount Orohena, its tallest peak, towers 2,241 metres above the sea right behind Papeete.
About 95% of the world's black pearls are grown in French Polynesia inside special black-lipped oysters that live in the warm lagoons.
The first Tahitians were brave Polynesian sailors who reached these islands over 2,000 years ago using only stars, waves and birds to find their way.
People in Papeete speak both French and Tahitian, and a friendly 'Ia Orana' (hello) is heard everywhere on the island.
Tahiti has black-sand beaches because the sand is made from old volcano lava that the waves have crushed into tiny shiny grains.
thingsToDo: Wander the colourful Marché de Papeete market and sniff vanilla pods, tropical fruit and flower crowns called couronnes.
Climb up to the white Pointe Vénus lighthouse and dig your toes into the soft black volcanic sand.
Look up at the twin red roofs of Notre-Dame Cathedral, the oldest Catholic church in the South Pacific.
Hike through the rainforest to splashy Fautaua Falls, one of the tallest waterfalls in the world.
Watch the sea shoot up like a fountain from the Arahoho Blowhole when big waves crash into the cliff.
Gaze up at Mount Orohena, Tahiti's huge volcanic peak, often hidden in soft white clouds.

wildlife.name: Tahiti Monarch
wildlife.description: Tiny shiny black bird found only on Tahiti, with fewer than 100 left in the wild.

wildlife.name: Green Sea Turtle
wildlife.description: Big gentle reptile that glides through the lagoons munching seagrass and seaweed.

wildlife.name: Blacktip Reef Shark
wildlife.description: Friendly small shark with black-tipped fins that swims in the shallow coral reefs.

wildlife.name: Polynesian Sandpiper
wildlife.description: Speckled brown shorebird that hops along Polynesian beaches hunting for tiny insects.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Tahitian — a friendly greeting used all day long.

images.alt: Inside the colourful Marché de Papeete market with stalls full of flowers and goods.
The twin red roofs of Notre-Dame Cathedral of Papeete on a sunny day.
The white Pointe Vénus lighthouse standing tall on a black-sand beach in Tahiti.
Fautaua Falls plunging down a tall cliff in the green Tahitian jungle.
The cloud-topped peaks of Mount Orohena and Mount Aorai rising above Tahiti.
The Arahoho Blowhole on Tahiti's coast, with sea spray bursting up through the rocks.
A small shiny black Tahiti monarch bird perched in a leafy tree.
A green sea turtle swimming over a coral reef in clear blue water near Tahiti.
A blacktip reef shark with a dark fin tip cruising over the lagoon floor at Moorea.
A speckled brown Polynesian sandpiper standing on a tree branch.
